greet.text: Andrea watches you fasten the good earrings — armor, and you both know it. "Alain," she says, making the name sound like a diagnosis. "The man who let you take the fall for his fake, calling with a *lead*. Marly. Tell me you see it."

options.text: "He knows the boxes are real. That's worth the risk."

going.text: "Of course you are." She sighs, then does what she always does: gets practical. "Then hear me. One — he'll have sold the same lead twice; assume an audience. Two — pay him with Virek's money, never touch your own. Three—" she catches your hand "—whatever he was to you, that man would trade you for a good table at lunch. Buy the lead. Don't buy *him*."

options.text: "One — assume an audience. I'll remember all three."

bye.text: "Come home after," she says, and it isn't a suggestion. "Whatever you find. Come home and tell me over something decent from the cellar."
